commit.message:
Add --output-junit flag for JUnit XML CI test reporting (#94)

Add a new --output-junit <path> CLI flag that writes a standard JUnit XML
test report alongside normal console output. This enables native test result
display in GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps, and GitLab CI pipelines.

Changes:
- AlRunner/JUnitReport.cs: new static class with WriteJUnit() method.
  Groups tests by codeunit name as <testsuite> elements.
  Uses <failure> for assertion failures, <error> for runner limitations.
  Time values are in seconds (invariant decimal separator).
- AlRunner/Pipeline.cs: add OutputJunitPath to PipelineOptions; add
  CodeunitName to TestResult (populated from SourceFileMapper); call
  JUnitReport.WriteJUnit() when OutputJunitPath is set.
- AlRunner/Program.cs: parse --output-junit <path> CLI flag; update
  --help text; update PrintGuide() with JUnit XML section.
- AlRunner.Tests/JUnitOutputTests.cs: 9 xUnit tests covering unit and
  integration behaviour.
- CHANGELOG.md: document the new feature.

Closes #72

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deduplicate repeated error blocks in PrintResults output (#70) (#95)

When many tests are blocked by the same runner error, PrintResults
previously repeated the identical error message block once per test,
generating hundreds of lines of noise in the output.

Changes:
- Add bool verbose = false parameter to Executor.PrintResults
- Group Error tests by message; if a message appears >= 2 times, print
  it once as a WARN block with a count, then print compact
  'ERROR TestName (blocked)' lines for each affected test
- With -v / verbose=true, fall back to full per-test detail (no dedup)
- Single/unique errors still show full detail (no WARN, no dedup)
- FAIL tests are never deduplicated
- Pipeline.cs passes options.Verbose to PrintResults

Before (66 blocked tests, same error):
  ERROR Test1
        CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
        Inject this dependency via an AL interface.
  ERROR Test2
        CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
  ... (66 × 3 lines = 198 lines of noise)

After:
  WARN  CompilationExcludedException: Codeunit 50123 was excluded...
        (66 tests blocked — use -v for per-test details)

  ERROR Test1 (blocked)
  ERROR Test2 (blocked)
  ... (3 + 66 lines total)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix deprecated Keras/TF API calls in deep learning notebooks

lectures/05a_deep_learning.ipynb:
- Cell 25: replace deprecated 'from tensorflow.keras import layers, Model'
  with 'from keras import layers, Model' (Keras 3.x standalone API)

lectures/05b_deep_learning.ipynb:
- Cell 1: remove 'import tensorflow as tf' (TF not in requirements)
- Cell 3 (sampling func): replace tf.random.normal/tf.shape/tf.keras.backend.exp
  with keras.random.normal/keras.ops.shape/keras.ops.exp
- Cells 5 & 7 (CustomVariationalLayer): replace all tf.keras.backend.*
  with keras.ops.* equivalents:
    tf.keras.backend.flatten(x) -> keras.ops.reshape(x, (-1,))
    tf.keras.backend.mean(...)  -> keras.ops.mean(...)
    tf.keras.backend.square(x) -> keras.ops.square(x)
    tf.keras.backend.exp(x)    -> keras.ops.exp(x)

All changes target Keras 3.x (keras==3.13.2) which removed the legacy
keras.backend functional API in favor of keras.ops (numpy-style ops).

commit.message:
fix: remove session-state directory from disk on session delete

Previously, deleting a session only called the SDK's deleteSession and
invalidated caches, but left the ~/.copilot/session-state/<id>/ directory
on disk. Since listSessionsFromDisk() scans these directories, deleted
sessions would reappear as phantom quick chats with no content.

Now deleteSession() also rm's the disk directory and clears the
visibleActivityCache entry for the session.

Three fixes for the hanging E2E test run:

1. AppHost.cs: check GRAPHHOPPER_PREBUILT_IMAGE env var — if set, use
   AddContainer() with the pre-built image instead of AddDockerfile().
   This skips the ~5-minute Dockerfile build that was causing the hang.
   Also reduces JAVA_OPTS heap to 2GB in CI (IoW graph is tiny).

2. AspireFixture.cs: add 8-minute CancellationTokenSource covering both
   BuildAsync and StartAsync. Previously only WaitForResourceAsync was
   covered, so a hung image build would wait forever.

3. ci.yml: set GRAPHHOPPER_PREBUILT_IMAGE=graphhopper-ci on the test step,

feat: -ignore-host flag to exclude specific hosts from all proxy rewriting

Problem: string replacements are applied to all upstream traffic, corrupting
JS bundles that contain the target string in functional code (e.g. the MSAL
auth library embeds "microsoft-graph" and "microsoft-profile-access" as
OAuth2 scope IDs; replacing "microsoft" with "msctf" breaks the SPA).

New flag:
  -ignore-host login.microsoftonline.com,graph.microsoft.com
  (repeatable; comma-separated)

Behaviour for ignored hosts:
- Request: path, query, headers, body skipped for all rewriting
           (rep.ToOriginal + unmaskRequestString not applied)
- Response: CSP rewriting, header masking, body masking all skipped
- Always runs (security): rewriteSetCookies, HSTS/key-pin stripping

Implementation:
- isIgnoredHost(host, map) helper — O(1) lookup, port-stripping, case-insensitive
- parseIgnoreHosts([]string) — validates and normalises host tokens at startup
  (no scheme, no path, port stripped, lowercased); called once before serving
- Director: skips path/query replacement (with subdomain-path peek to detect
  ignored subdomain hosts before extraction) and header/body rewrites
- ModifyResponse: early-return after security-mandatory cleanup for ignored hosts
- Startup log: lists all ignored hosts sorted (deterministic output)

Example:
  maskproxy -target microsoft.com -replace microsoft:msctf             -ignore-host login.microsoftonline.com             -ignore-host graph.microsoft.com

87 tests pass, race-clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix delta external table schema diff, script syntax, and cleanup (#164)

* Fix delta external table schema diff, script syntax, and cleanup

Three fixes for delta external tables:

1. Schema perpetual diff: The bulk loader populates the schema for all
   external tables from the cluster, but delta tables auto-infer their
   schema from the delta log — so YAML configs intentionally omit it.
   This caused a perpetual diff on every run. Fix: before comparing,
   clear the cluster-side schema for delta tables when the YAML doesn't
   specify one. If the YAML provides a custom schema, keep it for
   proper comparison.

2. Missing closing paren: CreateDeltaScript generated an unclosed
   parenthesis around the connection string, producing invalid KQL
   that fails Kusto syntax validation and cannot be applied.

3. Remove unnecessary dataFormat requirement: Delta tables don't use
   dataformat= in the Kusto command syntax. Removed the misleading
   validation that required it.

4. Only emit with() properties when set: Delta script no longer emits
   empty folder='', docString='', fileExtension='' when not specified
   in YAML. The with() block is omitted entirely if no properties are
   set.

Fixes github/data#11973

commit.message:
Add aspire destroy command for tearing down deployed environments (#16097)

* Add aspire destroy command for tearing down deployed environments

Implements #13013 - adds a top-level 'aspire destroy' command that tears down
previously deployed Aspire environments. The command follows the same pipeline
architecture as 'aspire deploy' and 'aspire publish'.

Changes:
- Add WellKnownPipelineSteps.Destroy and DestroyPrereq aggregation steps
- Add DestroyCommand CLI command with --yes flag to skip confirmation
- Add destroy-prereq step with interactive confirmation prompt
- Wire Docker Compose's existing docker-compose-down step to destroy
- Wire Kubernetes Helm's existing helm-uninstall step to destroy
- Add Azure resource group deletion via ARM SDK for ACA/App Service
- Add IResourceGroupResource.DeleteAsync to provisioning abstractions
- Add PipelineOptions.Yes for forwarding --yes flag to AppHost
- Update pipeline step count test and accept diagnostics snapshots

Validated with pipeline tests (65), Docker Compose tests (85),
Kubernetes tests (88), and Azure deployer tests (28) all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify destroy-prereq confirmation message

Keep the prereq generic — each environment step already surfaces
target-specific details (resource group, Helm release, compose project).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enumerate Azure resources before destroying resource group

Query the resource group via ARM to list all resources before deleting,
so users can see exactly what will be destroyed. The discovery phase
logs each resource type and name, then reports the total count.

Pipeline output:
  Discovering resources in myapp-rg
    ContainerApps/containerApps: api
    KeyVault/vaults: kv-myapp
    ContainerRegistry/registries: acrmyapp
  Found 3 resource(s) in myapp-rg
  Deleting resource group myapp-rg (3 resource(s))

If enumeration fails (e.g. permissions), deletion still proceeds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- DestroyCommandTests: help, invalid project, --step destroy argument,
  --yes flag forwarding, --output-path inclusion (5 tests)
- K8s: HelmUninstallStep_RequiredByDestroy verifies helm-uninstall
  depends on destroy-prereq
- Register DestroyCommand in test DI (CliTestHelper.cs)

Each environment step now owns its own confirmation with full context:
- Azure: discovers resources in RG, then asks to confirm deletion
- Docker Compose: asks to confirm compose down
- Kubernetes: asks to confirm helm uninstall with release name + namespace

Pipeline layering: destroy → destroy-{env} (prompt) → action step (no prompt)
This means 'aspire do docker-compose-down' skips the prompt (explicit action),
while 'aspire destroy' chains through the confirmation layer.

The generic destroy-prereq is now a plain no-op placeholder step.
--yes skips all confirmation prompts.

commit.message:
Enable fork PR support and fix race condition in review workflows (#38105)

## What

Switch all 7 review workflows (archie, dash, dexter, mgmt-review,
scribe, sentinel, tester) from `pull_request` to `pull_request_target`
to enable reviews on fork PRs. This follows the pattern established in
[azure-rest-api-specs#42268](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42268).

## Changes

- **`pull_request` → `pull_request_target`** with `forks: ["*"]` —
allows workflows to run on fork PRs
- **`workflow_dispatch`** with `item_number` input — enables manual
re-runs
- **Label removal step** in `on.steps` (pre-activation) — removes the
trigger label since `label_command` isn't used with
`pull_request_target`
- **Removed `fork == false` guard** from `if:` conditions — no longer
needed since the label gate (only collaborators can apply labels)
provides the security boundary
- **Label-aware concurrency group** — includes `github.event.label.name`
in the concurrency key to prevent a race condition where applying
multiple review labels simultaneously causes one workflow run to cancel
another (see #38093)

## Security

`pull_request_target` runs from the base branch context with write token
access. This is safe because:
1. Only collaborators can apply trigger labels (label gate)
2. The workflows read PR content via API calls, not by checking out fork
code
3. No untrusted code from the fork is executed

commit.message:
Phase 3: NodeRejected ClusterEvent and NodeRejectionReason (#143)

* feat: add NodeRejected ClusterEvent and NodeRejectionReason

Phase 3 of the version-compatibility implementation plan.

Extends ClusterEvent with a NodeRejected variant emitted when a node
fails the version handshake or the handshake transport call fails.

cluster.rs:
- ClusterEvent::NodeRejected { node_id, reason, detail }
- NodeRejectionReason enum: IncompatibleProtocol, IncompatibleAdapter,
  ConnectionFailed
- NodeRejectionReason is separate from handshake-level RejectionReason
  to avoid coupling cluster events to wire protocol internals
- From<RejectionReason> for NodeRejectionReason conversion
- Display impl for NodeRejectionReason
- Serde derives (conditional on serde feature)
- 6 unit tests: construction, emitter delivery, From conversion,
  Display, ConnectionFailed variant, equality

lib.rs:
- Re-export NodeRejectionReason

Design decisions:
- NodeRejectionReason lives in cluster.rs (not system_actors.rs) to
  keep cluster events independent from wire protocol types
- ConnectionFailed is cluster-level only (not in handshake proto) since
  it represents local transport failure, not a remote rejection
- From<RejectionReason> bridges the handshake domain to cluster domain

Zero clippy warnings across full workspace. All workspace tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: log WebSocket frames by default, -ws-no-log to suppress

After a WebSocket upgrade (101 Switching Protocols), httputil's bidirectional
copy bypasses ModifyResponse entirely. To give operators visibility into WS
traffic without buffering or payload modification, frame headers are now parsed
and logged as they stream through the proxy.

Implementation:
- wsLoggingTransport: wraps the HTTP transport; intercepts 101 responses and
  replaces resp.Body with a wsLoggingConn
- wsLoggingConn: stateful io.ReadWriteCloser wrapper that parses WS frame
  headers in both directions with zero buffering (bytes pass through unchanged)
- wsFrameParseState: per-direction header accumulator; handles arbitrary chunk
  sizes across Read/Write calls; supports short (≤125 B), 16-bit, and 64-bit
  payload lengths, masked frames, control frames, and fragmented frames
- Logger.LogWSFrame: logs opcode name, conn ID, direction, payload length,
  masked and fragment flags; no-op when logWS=false

Log output example:
  WS↕  conn#1 established
  WS↑  conn#1  op=text    len=42 [masked]
  WS↓  conn#1  op=text    len=38
  WS↓  conn#1  op=ping    len=0

Flags:
  (default)    WS frames logged to stderr
  -ws-no-log   suppress WS frame logging
